=== SHEET: How to Read This Document ===
"This workbook presents a structured analysis of OncoHealth Azure DevOps work items, showing where client-specific customizations occur across domains, features, and payer implementations.
Each sheet focuses on a specific analytical view",,
Sheet Name,What It Is,How to Read / Key Insights
ADO Work Items (Raw),"The source dataset of all 2,916 Azure DevOps work items with columns like Work Item ID, Type, Client, Domain / Functional Area, Feature, Parent ID / Description, State, and Created Date.","Use to validate pivot counts or investigate outliers.
About “Unclassified” items:  “Unclassified” work items  appears in Domain or Feature columns. These rows represent work that lacks a domain or feature tag in ADO - often cross-domain, infrastructure, or integration-related items. Potential causes:
- Cross-cutting work (integrations, infrastructure, admin, or analytics enablers).
- Items spanning multiple domains.
- Newly added or legacy tickets pending tagging."
Dataset Overview,"Narrative cover page with dataset scope and summary pivots by Type, Domain, Feature, and Client.",Use this as the executive summary to understand total work distribution and customization ratio (~78% Baseline / 22% Custom). Review short “Purpose / Interpretation” notes to see where the largest development effort occurs.
Domain / Functional Area Insight,"Table by Domain / Functional Area showing % Items that are Custom (“% (custom/total)”), Top Features Customized, Clients Affected, and Key Observations.","Identify which business areas show the highest customization intensity. How to read:
-  % Items that are Custom = customization ratio per domain.
- Top Features Customized = key feature hotspots.
- Clients Affected = breadth of impact.
- Key Observations = why variability occurs (e.g., rule or integration complexity)."
Feature Insights,"Narrative summary of Feature Adoption, Baseline vs Custom Split, and Unique Clients per Feature.","Highlights which features are reused vs. frequently customized. Features with high customization shares (e.g., rules engine, PBM integrations) indicate areas for configuration or modularization."
Client-Level Analysis,"Comparative table of % Work Custom per Client, along with Top Customized Domains, Top Customized Features, and Notable Patterns.","Compare client complexity levels. How to read:
- % Work Custom = client-specific adaptation level.
- Top Customized Domains/Features = where client divergence occurs.
- Notable Patterns = brief context."

128422,User Story,Blue KC Follow Up Requests,"Split all financial model components between self-insured, fully-insured, ACA, and MA. They probably want a table that shows savings, split by feature (chemo, xrt, soc, vial rounding ) by trg lob. Definitions and logic (including any diagnosis, procedure, revenue, place of service codes) used to determine the claims that you expect to be able to impact. Work with clinical to provide them our recommended master inscope list? As far as diagnosis, we can re-send the cancer diag list we send along the specs. Counterfactual trend used to determine savings in future years. Expected trend after engagement. Confirm that trends are gross of any rebates received from PBM or manufacturer. They are based on the claims data, which typically exclude any rebates, etc. So yes, Any supporting financial models develop to support the ROI presented. A definition of our intervention rates? &nbsp; See below section for reference. &nbsp; Provide details on any performance guarantee provisions. If any fees are at risk, please provide the explicit methodology used to validate performance. Provide assumption on conversion rate of drugs to biosimilars. we didn t tailor the biosim savings to their preferred drug list. We just use our blended rates. From a previous analysis : Keep in mind, certain health plans may have different preferred drug or step therapy rules in place, especially with biosimilars, so the data you see here is a blend an average denial rate across all those plans where some may prefer the drug and others do not . Are there any volume requirements related to this proposal, or may we implement a la carte? If we can t implement all lines, does this change pricing? Is there a graduated timeline requirement at a minimum? Reminder to reiterate that our savings are based on reviewing all the chemotherapy drugs identified as inscope (both medical and pharmacy), if they start with a subset of the drugs, it will impact savings. &nbsp; &nbsp; How We Calculate Savings &nbsp; &nbsp; The algorithms are evidence-based, and because the oncology landscape evolves so rapidly, we use the trailing twelve months of prior authorization (PA) history as the data behind the models.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp; In claims analysis, the most important PA statistic about a drug is the intervention rate (IR), which is the number of opportunities in which we intervened &nbsp;to create savings divided by the total number of requests we processed. &nbsp; The goal of the claims analysis is to correlate the savings for each drug to its real-world intervention rate. We want the estimates to be conservative; and tending toward the performance of the average client aids in that. &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp; To calculate gross projected savings for a particular drug, we take the sum of allowed amounts for all claim lines at the drug level and multiply that sum by the intervention rate from Prior Auth data.&nbsp; We then adjust down the projected savings from the intervention rate to account for our historical experience with: &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Changes versus denials and withdrawals.&nbsp; Changes have a fraction of the savings of a denial or withdrawal, since we reduce the savings by the cost of the approved regimen the request was changed to. &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Subsequent submissions.&nbsp; Like changes, we reduce the savings of denials and withdrawals when a subsequent submission is approved within 90 days of the original submission. &nbsp; &nbsp; Our PA data tell us we need to reduce gross projected savings by 20% to account for changes and subsequent submissions to arrive at net projected savings. &nbsp;",,2024-05-10 22:30:12,Closed,Technology\Data and Analytics Team\Reporting and Analytics,Technology\2024 Q2\20240517,BlueKC; ImpactAnalysis,124449,BlueKC Impact Analysis (Bundle),BCBSKS,Formulary & Pharmacy Data,Data | Pharmacy Auths + PBM Integrations,Client Customization

Please contact the member s health plan for authorization.&quot; Requested updated message &quot;This case cannot be submitted because the treatment start date is more than Max Months in the Past 30 days in the past. 163467,User Story,BlueKC Client Solutions Client UAT - Show & Tell,"Business Need: &nbsp; For a new payer implementation it is essential to validate the configured workflows, data mappings and integrated systems with direct payer engagement prior to go-live. This provides the payer with hands-on visibility into system functionality, enables feedback loops for fine-tuning configurations, and serves as a formal checkpoint for acceptance of the end-to-end case processing. Background : As a part of our continued growth and strategic partnerships, we are onboarding a new client to our fully delegated services portfolio. 138777,User Story,BCBSM Engg TAT Create 24 hour Business Day,"BCBSM Commercial uses a different definition for business days: Weekends are skipped Observed Holidays are skipped A business day counts as 24 hours If a milestone starts at midnight of a workday or at any time during weekend / holiday, it ends at 23:59:59 e.g.,&nbsp; a 4-day milestone with the start on Tuesday 00:00 will have a deadline set to Friday 23:59:59; a 3 -day milestone with the start on &nbsp; Saturday (any hour/minute except for midnight) will have a deadline set to Wednesday&nbsp; 23:59:59 For example: A timer with 3BD starts on Tuesday 11/19 at 11am --&gt; The due date is Friday 11/22 at 11am A timer with 3BD starts on Wednesday 11/20 at 3pm --&gt; The due date is Monday 11/25 at 3pm A timer with 3BD starts on Tuesday 11/26 at 4am --&gt; The due date is Monday 12/2 at 4am We need to extend the existing configurations to allow for this functionality. Example document Important notes: Duration Units are used downstream in reporting and there will be an expectation that they get the same units (Business Day) We can potentially deviate from the existing business day paradigm since this seems to be a hybrid between business (skip these days) and calendar days (due in 24 hr increments)",,2024-09-20 14:05:19,Closed,Technology\Engineering\Everest,Technology\2024 Q4\24.10.23,NO QA; Sprint Demo,130584,BCBSM | Engg | TAT Configurations (Basic),BCBSM,Unclassified (Review Needed),Unclassified (Review Needed),Baseline Implementation

139337,User Story,BCBSM Commercial & Medicare Medical Benefit Withdrawals,"Background: &nbsp;OH will begin processing dismissals and withdrawals on behalf of BCBSM on 01/01/2025. THIS story will cover Withdrawals. See 141170 &nbsp;for Dismissals. Working definition of withdrawal v. dismissal: For Medicare, dismissals occur anytime we receive a request for PA, but the request doesn't end in final determination of approved/denied. A withdrawal for a Medicare member is a dismissal for the reason that the request or a portion of the request was withdrawn. This could be by P2P/CCF/NMI or just unprompted/voluntary by Provider, Member or AOR. For Commercial, the request was withdrawn prior to final determination. (Dismissal is Medicare related terminology only.) The withdrawal could be by P2P/CCF/NMI or just unprompted/voluntary by Provider, Member or AOR. As a &nbsp; MATIS configuration/engineering team member I want &nbsp; to upload the withdrawal letters that will generate as part of the BCBSM medical necessity review process so that &nbsp;the letter verbiage for provider and member complies with payer guidelines. Impacts: Payer: &nbsp;BCBSM Line of Business: &nbsp; Commercial &amp; Medicare (Medical benefit only) Referral Types:&nbsp; Chemo PRE-Determination, Medicare Dismissal for Reason: Full withdrawal --When the whole request is withdrawn all drugs appear on the dismissal letter. Procedure statuses are &quot;Withdrawn&quot;. PRE-Determination, Medicare Dismissal for Reason: Partial withdrawal --When a portion of the request is withdrawn, the approval or denial template goes out it only has the approved/denied drugs on it, and&nbsp; but it's appended by the &nbsp;at the same time, the withdrawal template is also created and only the withdrawn drugs appear on the withdrawn/dismissal letter. (This happens often in change from/change to scenarios.) CYS, 10/28/24 PRE-Determination, Commercial Full withdrawal: &nbsp; When the whole request is withdrawn. The case is statused as withdrawn but no letters go out.&nbsp;&nbsp; PRE-Determination, Commercial Partial withdrawal: &nbsp; When a portion of the request is withdrawn, the drug(s)/code(s) in question are statused as withdrawn or are part of the &quot;changed from&quot; section of the case. No separate withdrawal letter goes out. POST Denial, Commercial Appeal Withdrawal: When a 1st level appeal is submitted to us an is subsequently withdrawn. Use the Appeal-Withdrawn status in the appeals module. &nbsp;This one has a dependency on&nbsp; User Story 141052 : Engg BCBSM Delegated Appeals Create new Fax Template Types for Appeals Withdrawals POST Denial, Medicare Reopen Withdrawal: Not applicable to BCBSM. Plan treats reopen requests like appeals.&nbsp; NOTE: Per BCBSM via Lina today 10/17/2024, OH will not be responsible for REOPEN upheld, overturn, dismissals or withdrawals. The health plan treats reopen requests like appeal requests. Reopen requests should be redirected to the plan. The newly created Post Denial case is statused as Reconsideration Withdrawn and no letters go out.&nbsp;&nbsp; Requirements : &nbsp; Create/Format Notification&nbsp; Templates and Set up Recipient Configs as needed Configure &nbsp; Templates in MATIS. Configure &nbsp; Recipients in MATIS.&nbsp; Withdrawal letters go to Medicare member by mail, Ordering Provider by fax, (for Medicare HMO, it also goes to servicing provider, but that will be done manually by CRS, not configured)&nbsp; CYS, 10/22/2024: CRS will need to be trained that for Medicare HMO withdrawals, if the servicing provider fax is different from the ordering provider one, they should add the servicing provider fax number. The occurrence of servicing fax being different is historically very rare.&nbsp; Provider fax number is always the fax number that was input for the provider office contact. Provider letters are always generated in English.&nbsp; Medicare member withdrawal notifications are only generated in English at this time. Withdrawal notifications can be generated from within Chemo &nbsp; c a ses &nbsp; Resources: BCBSM Notification Requirement Template 20241002.xlsx BCBSM Template Stuff.xlsx MATIS Training Library New (Interim?) When this happens, OH needs to have a way to identify those members and send an OOS letter for the drugs that are billed under the benefit the member doesn't have, so that we don't inadvertently approve a drug the member does not have benefits for.&nbsp;&nbsp; Predecessor: 143278 &nbsp;will address the workflow and BRE rules (recommended actions/reasons).&nbsp; This story will address the OOS Member Letter requirements that accompany the newly created workflow.&nbsp; As a &nbsp;MATIS Letter config engineer I want &nbsp;to configure the OOS Member letter and recipients so that when the status &quot;OOS Member&quot; is selected, the letter template that goes out contains the correct drugs and is sent to the correct recipients. Impacts : Payer: BCBSM Line of Business: &nbsp;Commercial, Medicare (when the member has only one benefit, either medical only or pharmacy only) Referral Types: &nbsp;Chemo Notification impact: OOS Member letter Requirements: The OOS Member letter will include the determination column, and determination column will behave just like the OOS Medical Letter in the drugs and messaging it currently displays, except that for the drugs the member does not have the benefit for, the Member OOS Drug Message will appear. The configuration of the message that appears in the determination column will be via the newly created value &quot; Member OOS drug message&quot; &nbsp; system config): &nbsp;&quot; This member does not have benefit coverage for this drug with the health plan. Please contact the appropriate carrier for coverage. &quot; &nbsp; The OOS Member letter recipients will be ordering provider by fax and health plan by email at&nbsp; medicaloncology bcbsm.com&nbsp; The header on the letter should just say Out of Scope (not Out of Scope-Member).&nbsp; &nbsp; edited 12/19/2024 by CSwenson The message that displays just under the procedure table will indicate that the member does not have Part B or Part D benefits depending on the eligibility. If member does not have a medical benefit (RX only) then the message will say: &quot; This procedure isn t reviewed by OncoHealth directly. We forwarded the request to your health plan for review. The following issue exposing wrong patient data was identified, steps to reproduce: 1) User 1 and User 2 have the same Member ID and corresponding records are present in EligibilityDB 2) User 1 is present in OneUM - patient is created, data in OADB.dbo.Eligibility is present 3) User tries to introduce User 2 to OneUM using 'New Patient' logic: select 'BCBSMI' as a provider enter Member ID of User1 and User 2 to the corresponding field change focus - set cursor beyond Member ID field As a result: the system identifies that patient with this Member ID already exists BUT it's actually User 1, not User 2 as a consequence the system does not allow patient creation the data of User 1 is exposed in browser's DevTools/Network tab We need to allow patient creation in this case for BCBSMI only , and to achieve this we need to implement the following behavior: do not perform member search after only Member ID is entered - add DOB, first and last names, meaning that we need to change Member ID field behavior not to react on focus change. This SQL script is designed to update a database related to healthcare payer formularies.&nbsp; Here's a high-level overview of what it does: HCPCS Codes : The script starts by declaring a table variable HCPCSCodes and populating it with a list of Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) codes. Temporary Table Creation : The script then creates a temporary table TempPayerFormulary to store PayerFormularyIDs. If the table already exists, it's dropped and recreated. Notes The&nbsp;Generic Product Identifier&nbsp;(GPI) is a 14-character&nbsp; hierarchical classification &nbsp;system created by Wolters Kluwer's Medi-Span that identifies drugs from their primary therapeutic use down to the unique interchangeable product regardless of manufacturer or package size. The code consists of seven subsets, each providing increasingly more specific information about a drug available with a prescription in the United States. The GPI is created and maintained by UpToDate, Inc a Wolters Kluwer Company.","Currently these are saved on the RXNavDenorm in DrugsDB and MATIS, we need to also save the NDC in&nbsp; OADB at the case level.&nbsp; Fields that need to be captured: NDC Saving of these data shall be performed upon&nbsp; saving &amp; submitting a case-on-Case Entry&nbsp; Dosing updates&nbsp; on Process Assignment &nbsp; protocol Change From/Change To action&nbsp; on Process Assignment &nbsp;",2025-02-12 17:46:14,Closed,Technology\Engineering\Monarch,Technology\2025 Q1\25.02.26,cherrypick0129; CPDone; SentaraPatch,148621,"Rx Enhancements | F3 | Capture selected branded drug or pack RXCUI for Rx Drugs (Must-Have | BCBSM, GEIS)",BCBSM,Formulary & Pharmacy Data,Data | Pharmacy Auths + PBM Integrations,Client Customization

Only applies to pharmacy drugs.&nbsp; Pharmacy Benefit Drug Review Training 07.31.2020 (3).pptx Clinical trial cases &nbsp; are administratively approved&nbsp;(not reviewed by OH clinicians) and the template used is the approval template. Reconsideration cases &nbsp; are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the approval template if the reconsideration outcome is &quot;overturned&quot;.&nbsp; If a previously denied request is submitted for reconsideration and the result is a Reconsideration Overturn, the approval letter is sent to member and provider. (Only applies to Commercial and Medicaid LOB.) Reopen cases &nbsp; are&nbsp; are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the approval template if the reopen outcome is &quot;overturned&quot;.&nbsp; If a previously denied request is submitted for reopen and the result is a Reopen Overturn, the approval letter is sent to member and provider. (Only applies to Medicare LOB.) NOTE :&nbsp; For BCBSM, pharmacy benefit drug determinations go on separate letter templates to accommodate different verbiage that applies to pharmacy drugs. Resources: Pharmacy Benefit Templates &nbsp;approved by Payer:&nbsp; Software Engineering - Medicare - All Documents Link to MATIS Workflow/Training:&nbsp; MATIS Training Library &nbsp; Link to BCBSM Requirements Docs:&nbsp;&nbsp; BCBSM Notification Requirement Template 20241002.xlsx Denial Scenario Glossary: Full denial &nbsp; is when all drugs (that are not PA exempt or OOS) in the case are denied . Partial denial &nbsp; (some plans call this a partial approval, but we call it partial denial) is when some drugs are approved, but others are denied, within the same case. The procedure table in the notification lists the appropriate status in the line that contains the drug. (OH partial approvals are approvals for a dose, duration or frequency that is less than what was originally requested.)&nbsp; Reconsideration &nbsp; cases are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template &nbsp;if &nbsp;the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Reopen &nbsp;cases &nbsp; cases are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template &nbsp;if &nbsp;the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Benefit Denial &nbsp; is generated when a request for treatment is medically necessary, but the member does not have out of network benefits and the treating provider is out of network.&nbsp; NOTE : &nbsp; For BCBSM, pharmacy benefit drug determinations go on separate letter templates to accommodate different verbiage that applies to pharmacy drugs. 142878,User Story,BCBSM Provider Data Performance Testing,"Performance Concern Historically, performance around provider related queries in Matis were slow. Primary reasons of that are: 1) Large amount of data in the corresponding tables (with over 1M records in each): sfOriginContact, sfOriginAccount User 2) The queries were written often without thinking about data growth in future (and thus performance deterioration).&nbsp; 3) DB schema was not indexed enough to support popular query requests. After the population of Michigan's provider data (huge dataset with 2.7M records to sfOriginContact, and 495K to sfOriginAccount), the overall performance of existing queries should decrease. The problem will be common all payers independently if they use Source field or not. We have to find all potentially performance-weak places in Matis UI related to provider data and create the stories for optimization. The severity of performance issues will be quite serious, since they may end up with DB timeout exceptions (500) after elapsing the 30 sec. Testing Scope 1) Select payer with Source field set (e.g.&nbsp; 'G') and verify performance operations for (by CRS and Provider roles): search ordering provider (by npi, taxId, first/last name) search treating provider (npi, tin, first/last name) search place of treatment (its direct query to sfOriginAccount with 4.2M records) on search patient - verify how quick patient is found and PHI info is filtered out (ordering provider / diagnosis, documents) discontinuation case provider dashboard - count for all tabs, data loading for each tab / pagination..., document popup loaded in Submitted/Completed tabs,&nbsp; other places accessing sfOriginContact / Account - e.g. User Management - Link Salesforce Contact . 2) For HP's SSO Provider - test search providers, PoT, check provider dashboard. 3) For HPHC's SSO Provider -&nbsp; test search providers, PoT, check provider dashboard. 4) For BCBSM SSO Provider - test search providers, PoT, check provider dahsboard. CHAPTER 19. LICENSING AND REGULATION OF INSURANCE PROFESSIONALS SUBCHAPTER R. UTILIZATION REVIEWS FOR HEALTH CARE PROVIDED UNDER A HEALTH BENEFIT PLAN OR HEALTH INSURANCE POLICY 28 TAC 19.1710, 19.1730 19.1733 OVERVIEW The Commissioner of Insurance adopts amended 28 TAC 19.1710 and new 28 TAC Chapter 19, Subchapter R, Division 2, 19.1730 19.1733, concerning requirements prior to issuing an adverse determination and preauthorization exemptions. These amended and new sections implement House Bill 3459, 87th Legislature, 2021. The Commissioner adopts 19.1710 without changes to the proposed text published in the April 8, 2022, issue of the Texas Register (47 TexReg 1856). This section will not be republished. The Commissioner adopts 19.1730 19.1733 with revisions made in response to public comments. These sections will be republished. Subsection (b) states that an issuer must review the outcomes of no fewer than five eligible preauthorization requests for a particular health care service in a given evaluation period and determine whether the physician or provider qualifies for an exemption. TDI specifically sought comments on this minimum threshold for review and in response to comments changes the proposed text to reduce it from 20 preauthorization requests to five eligible preauthorization requests. &nbsp; https://texreg.sos.state.tx.us/public/regviewer ext.RegPage?sl R&amp;app 8&amp;p dir &amp;p rloc 408585&amp;p tloc &amp;p ploc &amp;pg 1&amp;p reg 408585&amp;ti 28&amp;pt 1&amp;ch 19&amp;rl 1732&amp;issue 08/26/2022 &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; ASHLEY BREVDA CORPORATE COUNSEL/CHIEF COMPLIANCE OFFICER t: &nbsp; 954.330.0142 x 218 f: &nbsp; 800.264.6128 w: &nbsp; www.oncohealth.us e: &nbsp; abrevda oncohealth.us &nbsp; &nbsp; This email may contain confidential and/or privileged material. 154183,User Story,"MATIS BSWHP, GEIS, Sentara ZD 94663 Apply newly updated procedure table from ADO 153548 to XRT letters","Background : As part of a recent enhancement to deny individual XRT codes, we introduced a new column in the XRT procedure table called &quot;Approved Quantity&quot;. The way it should have functioned was that the requested quantity would appear in the existing &quot;Quantity&quot; column and the approved quantity would appear in the approved quantity column. It didn't work quite how we envisioned it and the prod version of the enhancement is currently causing abrasion for providers and payers. So, we're enhancing that logic to make things better. Predecessor: 153548 As a &nbsp;MATIS letter engineer&nbsp; I want&nbsp; to reapply the newly updated procedure table. so that &nbsp;the quantity of any individual code that is being approved or denied is very clearly indicated to payers and providers. For approved codes the value that appears in the Quantity column is the max approvable value from the UI. &nbsp; For denied codes that appear in the Denied Billing Codes section of the letter, the value that appears in the Quantity column is the amount from Denied Quantity in the UI &nbsp;It&nbsp; should work like this on these: For Benefit Denials, use the MAX approvable value in the Quantity column. For Partial Denials, when the Main is approved but the Boost is not, or vice versa, use the&nbsp;MAX approvable value in the Quantity column. For Full Denials, use the MAX approvable value in the Quantity column. For Fully Withdrawn XRT cases, use the MAX approvable value in the Quantity column. For Partially Withdrawn XRT cases (main approved or denied but boost is withdrawn),&nbsp;use the MAX approvable value in the Quantity column. Not needed at all:&nbsp; For Partially Withdrawn XRT cases where the QTY that exceeds MAX approvable is withdrawn, display the value that exceeds MAX approvable. 170458,User Story,Matis Tech Enhance SSO Payer Access login,"Technical Requirements 1) Document DB schema for SSO Payer Access 2) During login of Payer User via SSO existing algorithm auto-selects first user with provided User Email. That's ok for actual user on PROD, but creates inconveniences for internal testing / debugging, since all internal users have several roles. As a workaround, all other roles are soft-deleted for some time, and later reverted back. First of all it takes extra time for such manipulations, and also requires us to remember this hacky workaround. And normally, you don't remember, gets stuck - ask developer to look in the code, etc. So, it's takes time of several resource just to recollect that workaround. Long story short, that should be improved: once we know that user is coming in the SSO Payer Access role, we'll pick all his role per email, and will select first role matching payer access naming convention. Existing naming convention for Payer Access users: User Group: PayerName Payer Access Security Group: &nbsp; PayerName Payer Access Pick the the first role with such naming convention by joining User.UserGroupID to LookupElelemnt and checking Text field, it should end with Payer Access . We intentionally don't check for exact PayerName, since it may deviates sometimes (e.g. the nick name of the payer was used, or some shorter version).","Small Regression testing: ensure that user with 1 role still can normally logic. Check this for all existing SSO Payers: Geisinger, BlueKC, Cambia verify that internal users with multiple role are normally logged in as SSO Payer Access user for given PayerID (coming from Okta token) if internal user has several SSO payer user roles, then auto-selection logic will pick 1st one. That still will most likely provide access to the cases of all payers, since such user should be mapped to these payers in the User MemberAssociation &nbsp;and PayerOrganization . Just check this scenario and document in the story comment.",2025-09-25 09:56:51,Active,Technology\Engineering\Dragons,Technology\2025 Q4\25.10.08,NOT COMMITTED,161102,"Cambia | Engg, Data | Provider & Payer SSO Configuration",Cambia,Admin & Operations,Engg | User Access Management,Baseline Implementation

132673,User Story,GEIS Engg Create secondary view for reporting consumption of TAT events (TAT API overhaul 3 of 3),"We need to create views for reporting so that they can consume TAT data. This will consist of 3 separate views: dbo.vw CaseInformation &nbsp;- Case information - 1 row 1 case dbo.vw CaseEvents &nbsp;- Case events - 1 row 1 case There are certain instances when we can have more than one event of a given type associated with a case for TAT. Generally other events of that type are soft deleted, but in some cases they may not be. In cases where more than one non-deleted event exist, reporting will need for us to provide the most recently created timestamp'ed&nbsp; event &nbsp;and in other instances the earliest event timestamp . See below for which to use when. in order to pull new notification events into the current view, an assignment should be created after 2024-12-30 (hardcoded in view for now), to alter this date for testing the view should be altered with new value. dbo.vw CaseTimers - Case timers - N row(s) per 1 case N can be 0 if the case has no timers associated with it Michael Sparace Do we need another view to list all notification events within assignments (multiple rows per assignment)? As a &nbsp; Genesis config team member,&nbsp; I want &nbsp; to identify, upload and link the appropriate CHIP notification documents in MATIS and configure recipients so that &nbsp; when an CHIP request is finalized, the notification is generated and delivered seamlessly to appropriate parties. Impacts: Payer: &nbsp;Geisinger Line of Business: &nbsp; &nbsp; CHIP, medical benefit only Referral Types: Denials LOMN LOI Partial Denials (some drugs approved, some drugs denied) Benefit (Admin) Denials (provider OON and member has no OON benefits) Reconsideration Upheld (reconsiderations are only for Medicaid/CHIP/Commercial, reopens are N/A, since reopens are only applicable to Medicare). Requirements&nbsp; Create/Format Denial Notification&nbsp; Templates and Set up Recipient Configs as needed Provider and Member&nbsp;Templates are present &nbsp;in MATIS. Recipients are configured &nbsp;in MATIS.&nbsp; Denial letters go to Member by mail, Provider by fax. (See ADO 130484 for member preferred language on demand requirements.) Member notifications are preceded by a mailing vendor-required cover page. Denial notifications can be generated from within cases for all&nbsp;members.&nbsp; Full denial is when all drugs (that are not PA exempt or OOS) in the case are denied. Partial denial is when some drugs are approved and some drugs are denied in the case. Reopen cases are performed only for Medicare LOB, so N/A for this story. Member address is pre-populated in the patient demographic section on the Start a Prior Authorization page. Provider fax number is always the fax number that was input for the provider office contact. Provider and member letters are in English. Provider and member letters have no formatting issues or other unexpected issues. Provider letter has OH fax cover page. Member letter has OH KS mailing vendor cover page. This extends the total TAT but should not exceed &nbsp; 5 days &nbsp;in total. This determination step includes all necessary notification POST SERVICE Pre-condition: the case is created for Geisinger&nbsp; Commercial POS&nbsp; Post Service &nbsp;The total TAT for a case must not exceed &nbsp; 30 days &nbsp; from the date of submission. 131056,User Story,SPIKE GEIS 2 Engg Enhance TAT Stored Proc Evaluate Historical documents and notification method for historical cases,"Need to evaluate historical documents and notification methods for historical cases. Identify the impacts/dependencies for the new events needed for 128726 &nbsp; Background: What document type and notification methods satisfy the existing requirements for a withdrawal having been completed for TAT / Compliance purposes? Does this vary by payer / MLOB? Does this logic live somewhere today I can reference? Tim Response:&nbsp; Yes, it does vary by payer/lob and add in delegation level. You should not trust our current logic. It's years old and probably not good. Basically, you should go through every payer/LOB and see what letters go out for withdrawals, and to who. That will determine when the TAT clock stops . For example: A fully delegated Medicare withdrawal case will have a member notification go out. That will stop the TAT clock A partially delegated Medicare withdrawal will have a payer notification go out. That will stop the TAT clock A fully delegated commercial withdrawal may not have a member notification go out, therefore the payer notification would stop the TAT clock. But another payer might require member notifications and the member notification would stop the TAT clock What document type and notification methods satisfy the existing requirements for a OOS having been completed for TAT / Compliance purposes? Does this vary by payer / MLOB? Does this logic live somewhere today I can reference? Tim Response:&nbsp; OOS cases tend to end at payer notification, but again, the templates for a given payer/lob should determine that. It really all depends on what templates are sent out. Do we have documented the type of notifications required and to whom for post denial cases for TAT purposes? If not I think it might be a candidate we can add to a tracker Lauren &nbsp;is creating for Post Denial Workflow items as a whole.&nbsp; Tim Response:&nbsp; Same for post denials. It should key off what templates are set up. Notice how I kept talking about letter templates. This was the exercise we undertook for SmartUM payers to determine what stops the TAT clock. Knowing what letters go out makes it a lot easier to determine when a TAT clock stops. If a member letter goes out for a specific payer/lob, that will be the TAT end, but if no member letter goes out, the payer notification usually becomes the TAT end.",,2024-06-13 19:59:52,Closed,Technology\Engineering\Genesis\Client Implementations,Technology\2024 Q3\24.08.28,,123786,Geisinger | TAT | Engg | Enhanced TAT (Go Live 2025 Q1),Geisinger,Notifications & Alerts,Engg | Chemo/XRT Notifications,Baseline Implementation

134652,User Story,GEIS Enhanced TAT Pass additional metadata for notification events and log them (New Events 1 of 3),"Anytime a document is sent, we should trigger a new event (&quot; NotificationDelivered &quot;) to the Tat-event topic on the Azure Service Bus. This event should contain all the existing parameters they already have today in addition to 4 new attributes: notificationRecipientType - who is receiving the document. We should create new lookup elements that have the following values: Member or Member Representative Ordering Provider Treating Provider Referring PCP&nbsp; (note: should determine if valid and remove from scope if not / unknown - no configurations leverage this today) Place of Treatment&nbsp; &nbsp; (note: should determine if valid and remove from scope if not / unknown - no configurations leverage this today) Payer notificationMethod &nbsp;- how that document is being transmitted. Partial denial is when some drugs are approved, but others are denied, within the same case. The procedure table in the notification lists the appropriate status in the line that contains the drug. Reconsideration cases are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template if the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Reconsideration cases are new cases generated in M ATIS using the same XRT codes from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template &nbsp; if &nbsp; the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Reopen cases are performed only for Medicare LOB, so N/A for this story. Background :&nbsp; As part of GEIS provider data creation process, there was a logic implemented in which system saves the Provider Info in database, with null Provider ID. In such scenarios, the auth file generation process was expected to fill in NULL provider IDs with payer defined default value. It is possible that in some scenarios, instead of persisting a NULL Provider ID, system is saving SalesForce Provider ID in database for new provider data creation. Hence for such scenarios, the&nbsp; auth file process wont set the default Provider ID, but will use the SF Provider ID SF Provider IDs are OH generated ones which are not known to the Payer and hence getting rejected as invalid Provider ID Steps to recreate :&nbsp; Simulate one of the PROD cases in non-PROD environment Identify the scenario which is saving SF Provider ID (It might be happening only for facility - sfaccount). Resources: &nbsp; Link to Medicaid&nbsp; Medical&nbsp; Benefit Templates &nbsp;approved by Payer: &nbsp; Medicaid Link to LOB &amp; Product Tab in the Client Guid e :&nbsp; OneUM Client Guide 052024.xlsx Link to MATIS reconsiderations training :&nbsp; Reconsideration Interim Training Powerpoint 070120 Link to MATIS denial training: &nbsp; Denials Training 27Jun2019.pptx Link to Geisinger Inventory:&nbsp; Geisinger Implementation Notification Requirement Template.xlsx (sharepoint.com) Requirements&nbsp; Create/Format Denial Notification&nbsp; Templates and Set up Recipient Configs as needed Provider and Member&nbsp;Templates are present &nbsp;in MATIS. Recipients are configured &nbsp;in MATIS. &nbsp; Member, Ordering Provider, Servicing Provider Denial letters go to Member by mail, Provider by fax. Pre-requisite: Member address is pre-populated in the patient demographic section on the Start a Prior Authorization page (from eligibility or by manual entry). Pre-requisite: Provider fax number field is populated with a viable fax number. Provider fax number is always the fax number that was input for the provider office contact. Provider letters are always generated in English.&nbsp; Member notifications are only generated in English at this time. (See ADO 130484 for member preferred language on demand requirements.) Provider and Member notifications are preceded by a mailing vendor-required cover page. Provider and Member notifications include LOB specific appeal language.&nbsp; The appeal language that displays in the letters will vary depending on the product and carrier code combination that is applicable to the member's plan. Product Line and LOB are what will determine the applicable verbiage. Denial notifications can be generated from within Chemo&nbsp; c a ses for Medicaid&nbsp; members . &nbsp; Full denial is when all drugs (that are not PA exempt or OOS) in the case are denied . Partial denial is when some drugs are approved, but others are denied, within the same case. The procedure table in the notification lists the appropriate status in the line that contains the drug. Reconsideration cases are new cases generated in M ATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template &nbsp; if &nbsp; the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Benefit Denial is generated when a request for treatment is medically necessary, but the member does not have out of network benefits and the treating provider is out of network.&nbsp; NOTE: For Partial Denials the current state of the template is that the header says &quot;Complete Denial&quot;. We will need to replace that header info to say &quot;Partial Denial&quot; by&nbsp; replacing it with the DenialTypeLanguage variable which would change the verbiage on the header in that instance. Added 08/27/2024 CYS. Reconsideration cases are new cases generated in M ATIS using the same XRT codes from the originally denied case and the template used is the denial template &nbsp; if &nbsp; the reconsideration outcome is &quot;upheld&quot; . Reopen cases are performed only for Medicare LOB, so N/A for this story. 150479,User Story,MATIS GEIS Ignore Filtering PPPID in Network Check,"Application: MATIS Applicable Payers: GEIS&nbsp; LOB: All Area/ Service: Provider As a CRS user I should be able to submit prior auth for GEIS patients with INN network providers ( GEIS Provider INN OON Scenarios.xlsx ) so that correct determination can be made by Clinical Team.&nbsp; Background: Geisinger network checks within MATIS are not querying the provider info staging table correctly. &nbsp; This results in in-network items being marked as out-of-network. The issue is related to the PPP ID filtering, where mismatched or manually added records cause the PPP ID hash to not match the database ID.&nbsp; For Geisinger, the criteria should ignore the primary payer ID in the query to avoid the problem. This should be conditional and not a wholesale removal, as it is valid in other places. Possible Resolution ideas: adding a parameter to the app settings to ignore PPP ID in the query for Geisinger. This parameter would be conditional based on the payer name, preventing the primary payer ID from being appended to the query if it exists in the ignore list. The request model would reference the payer name, and if it exists in the ignore list, the primary payer ID would not be appended to the query. if other payers face similar issues in the future, they can be added to the exclusion list, making the solution adaptable and configurable. Prov Entity Type Type 1 Individual healthcare providers, such as doctors, dentists, and nurses. Identifies individual practitioners. It s like a personal identifier that follows the provider throughout their career, regardless of where they work. Example:&nbsp; A doctor working in multiple clinics will use the same Type 1 NPI at each location. Type 2 Organizations, such as hospitals, group practices, and nursing homes. Identifies healthcare organizations. This NPI is used for billing and administrative purposes to represent the entity providing the service. Example:&nbsp;A hospital or a group practice will have a Type 2 NPI that is used for all billing and claims related to the services provided by the organization . Prov Entity ID : GHP defined identifier to recognize the type of Provider Entity. Values are: G Group I IPA P Practitioner H Hospital","PPPID filtering should be removed and ignored for provider network checks within Geisinger Payer Restoring Dev environment from Prod &amp; review the affected cases &amp; verify network check issues are resolved.&nbsp; GEIS Provider INN OON Scenarios.xlsx Member's ProductOfferCode from Eligibility file should match Networkid within Providerinfostaging table for Ordering Provider to be considered INN for Servicing/Treating Provider to considered INN If no match occurs, provider will be considered as OON Please review attached spreadsheet",2025-02-08 04:55:15,Closed,Technology\Phoenix,Technology\2025 Q1\25.03.26,,147357,2025 Q1 | UM Prod Support | MATIS,Geisinger,Provider & Network Management,Data | Provider Data,Baseline Implementation

43551,User Story,"Commercial - Minnesota, North Dakota & Iowa configurations for Health Partners TAT","The following timer configurations need to be created then validated Commercial - Minnesota, North Dakota &amp; Iowa: FullCaseDeadline NoVariation HPTruncateToOfficeClose See the&nbsp; Timers&nbsp; and&nbsp; TimerConfig NONGEHA &nbsp;tabs in the&nbsp; TAT Timing matrix - WIP &nbsp;excel sheet for details on initiated by, revaluated by, completed by, and canceled by. In addition: The primary timer should also be set to:&nbsp;FullCaseDeadline The timer can go OOTF and should not expire This story is all normalized to eastern time , however HP is in central time. Link to spreadsheet The timer will use the configurations as a base and then truncate them further. Th e overall business goal is to ensure the following: OA always&nbsp;has at least 4 hours to process a case. Whenever possible those hours occur during OA Business hours: 8:30am EST / EDT to 8pm EST / EDT Monday - Friday 8:30am EST / EDT to 6pm EST / EDT Saturday HP always has at least 2-3 hours to mail the case (2 during weekends, 3 during weekdays). Those hours should occur during HP Business hours: 9am EST / EDT to 7pm EST / EDT Monday - Friday 9am EST / EDT to 5pm EST / EDT Saturday Otherwise times should be truncated to the previous day to the following times: If truncated to Monday - Friday: 4pm EST / EDT If truncated to Saturday: 3pm EST / EDT If truncated to Sunday: 12pm &nbsp;(noon)&nbsp; EST / EDT Should we not be able to truncate the time or allow them to have at least 2 hours of time during business hours CRS will need to use their paging service (Recommended Action story is separate from this) For this LOB / State combo: For STAT cases, OA has a maximum of 42 of the full 48 hours deadline For Standard cases, OA has a maximum of 3 calendar days of the full 5 calendar day deadline The above can be solved with the following rules: If a STAT case is started on: A Sunday through Wednesday: At or before 11:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the next day at 4pm After 11:00:00.000 and at or before&nbsp; 22:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 42 hours) After 22:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future at 4pm A Thursday: At or before 11:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the next day at 4pm After 11:00:00.000 and at or before&nbsp; 21:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 42 hours) After 21:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future at 3pm A Friday: At anytime --&gt; The case is truncated to the next day at 3pm A Saturday: At or before 11:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the same day at 3pm After 11:00:00.000 and at or before&nbsp; 22:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 42 hours) After 22:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future (Monday) at 4pm If a STANDARD case is started on: Any day / anytime --&gt; The case is due within 3 days (72 hours). No further truncation is needed. Note: This is because the longest amount of time HP is closed (44 hours) is less than the time they have allotted to mail the case (2 calendar days / 48 hours).","A single timer shows for Health Partners Commercial Non-ASO cases in Minnesota, North Dakota &amp; Iowa with the correct time",2021-05-27 23:18:36,Closed,Technology\Engineering\MATIS\Phoenix,Technology\2021 Q4\21.10.13,HealthPartners; TAT Overhaul,50580,Health Partners Partial Delegation - Truncated Timeframes and Afterhours On Call Paging (Associated CR),HealthPartners,Provider & Network Management,Data | Provider Data,Baseline Implementation

Otherwise times should be truncated to the previous day to the following times: If truncated to Monday - Friday: 4pm EST / EDT If truncated to Saturday: 3pm EST / EDT If truncated to Sunday: 12pm &nbsp;(noon)&nbsp; EST / EDT Should we not be able to truncate the time or allow them to have at least 2 hours of time during business hours CRS will need to use their paging service (Recommended Action story is separate from this) For this LOB / State combo: For STAT cases, OA has a maximum of 16 of the full 24 hours deadline For Standard Concurrent &amp; Pre-service cases, OA has a maximum of 60 hours of the full 72 hour deadline For Retrospective cases, OA has a maximum of 4 calendar days of the full 14 calendar day deadline The above can be solved with the following rules: If a &nbsp; STAT &nbsp; Concurrent / Preservice &nbsp; case is started on: A Monday through Thursday: At or before &nbsp; 12:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is truncated to the same day at 4pm After 12:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 16 hours) A Friday: At or before &nbsp; 12:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is truncated to the same day at 4pm After 12:00:00.000 and at or before &nbsp; 23:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 16 hours) After 23:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the next day at 3pm A Saturday: At or before &nbsp; 11:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is truncated to the same day at 3pm After 11:00:00.000 and at or before &nbsp; 20:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 16 hours) After 20:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the next day at 12pm (noon) A Sunday: At or before 8:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to the same day at 12pm (noon) After 8:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 16 hours) If a STANDARD Concurrent / Preservice case is started on: A Saturday through Tuesday: At or before 4:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) After 4:00:00.000 and at or before 1 2:00:00.000 &nbsp; --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future at 4pm After 12:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) A Wednesday: At or before 4:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) After 4:00:00.000 and at or before 11:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future at 3pm After 11:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) A Thursday: At or before 3:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) After 3:00:00.000 --&gt; The case is truncated to two days in the future at 3pm A Friday: At or before 12:00:00.000 --&gt; &nbsp; The case is truncated to the next day at 3pm After 12:00:00.000 0 &nbsp; --&gt; &nbsp; The case is not truncated (is due within 60 hours) If a Post-Service case is started on: Any day / anytime --&gt; The case is due within 4 days. No further truncation is needed. Note: This is because the longest amount of time HP is closed (44 hours) is less than the time they have allotted to mail the case (10 calendar days / 240 hours).",,2021-06-15 19:26:00,Closed,Technology\Engineering\MATIS\Phoenix,Technology\2021 Q4\21.10.27,HealthPartners; STRETCH; TAT Overhaul,50580,Health Partners Partial Delegation - Truncated Timeframes and Afterhours On Call Paging (Associated CR),HealthPartners,Provider & Network Management,Data | Provider Data,Baseline Implementation

146131,User Story,"PA Exempt drugs need to be removed from the Highmark PDL Denial report, PDL Approval report, and the QM UM 7 report","PA Exempt drugs need to be removed from the following reports: Gateway Medicaid PDL Approved Gateway Medicaid PDL Denied Gateway Medicaid QM UM 7 Denial Log Here is a sample query to identify if a drug is PA exempt. select from (select asu.OAReferenceNumber, asu.PatientWrittenNotificationDate, asu.PatientLastName, asu.PatientFirstName, asu.MemberID, asu.AssignmentInitiationDate , tade.HCPCSCode, tade.HCPCSCodeName, tad.ProtocolStatus , (select dc.InternalMessage from oadb.dbo.InsuranceProviderDrugConfiguration dc left join oadb.dbo.InsuranceProviderDrugConfiguration LOB dclob on dc.InsuranceProviderDrugConfigurationID dclob.InsuranceProviderDrugConfigurationID join oadb.dbo.Drug d on dc.DrugID d.DrugID left join oadb.dbo.LineofBusiness lob on dclob.LineOfBusinessID lob.LineOfBusinessID join oadb.dbo.InsuranceProvider ip on dc.InsuranceProviderID ip.InsuranceProviderID where 1 1 and (dclob.Deleted is null or dclob.Deleted 0) and (dc.Deleted is null or dc.Deleted 0) and (dc.InternalMessage like '%PA Exempt%') and asu.InsuranceProviderID ip.InsuranceProviderID and asu.PatientMajorLOB lob.LOBName and tade.HCPCSCodeID d.DrugID ) 'Is PA Exempt' from Analytical Services.dbo.AssignmentSummary asu join Analytical Services.dbo.TblAssignmentDetails tad on tad.PatientProtocolAssignmentID asu.PatientProtocolAssignmentID join Analytical Services.dbo.TblAssignmentByElement tade on tade.PatientProtocolAssignmentProtocolID tad.PatientProtocolAssignmentProtocolID where 1 1 and asu.InsuranceProviderID 14 and asu.PatientMajorLOB 'Medicaid' and asu.AssignmentInitiationDate &gt; '2024-12-01' -- Change the date... Should it be initiation or notification?? and asu.AssignmentInitiationDate &lt; '2025-01-01' -- Change the date... Should it be initiation or notification?? --and tad.ProtocolStatus like '%Deni%' and tad.ProtocolStatus not in ('Not Submitted for Authorization', 'Pending', 'Changed from - Original Protocol', 'Deleted', 'Withdrawn') ) pa where 1 1 and pa.Is PA Exempt is not null order by pa.MemberID, pa.PatientLastName, pa.PatientFirstName Notice this line: &quot; and pa.Is PA Exempt is not null &quot;. This is what is used to determine if the drug is PA exempt. This data does not currently exist in analytical services or in the EDP. It only exists in OADB as of today. There are two lines in the query to filter by a date range. You will want to use whatever date the report filters on. As of right now, I can't find any effective dates around the PA exempt drug. This means that we will have to assume all drugs in the PA exempt list in Matis have always been PA exempt. Having said that, there is a deleted flag on the table. You can see above it was used. What can, and should be done is, if the drug is decided before it was deleted, consider it PA exempt. If it is decided after the drug was deleted, do not consider it PA exempt.",,2024-12-09 17:31:15,Closed,Technology\Data and Analytics Team\Reporting and Analytics,Technology\2025 Q1\20250207,,,,Highmark,Formulary & Pharmacy Data,Data | Pharmacy Auths + PBM Integrations,Client Customization

8277,User Story,MCS Monthly Reports,"we have a set of scheduled monthly reports that includes: Activity Report Savings Report TAT Report Network Availability these reports are not automatically sent to the client because they need to be checked by me first to be sure that all pieces are in the right place like: Membership; I make sure that we received the eligibility file on time, sometimes we have to ask IT / Finance for it. Savings and protocol prices; I check if there are outliers numbers; if that's so, I dig the source of the problem and I ask PharmDs or Dr. Fishman to update the wrong values in MATIS for me to re run the report. Out of time cases, I send Operations a list of cases that were out of time to be sure they were really out of time; sometimes there's a wrong date in MATIS that made a case out of time when it shouldn't be. In that case Operations has to update the date in MATIS and then I re run the report to get the corrected data. 13065,User Story,MCS Monthly Reports,"we have a set of scheduled monthly reports that includes: Activity Report Savings Report TAT Report Network Availability these reports are not automatically sent to the client because they need to be checked by me first to be sure that all pieces are in the right place like: Membership; I make sure that we received the eligibility file on time, sometimes we have to ask IT / Finance for it. Savings and protocol prices; I check if there are outliers numbers; if that's so, I dig the source of the problem and I ask PharmDs or Dr. Fishman to update the wrong values in MATIS for me to re run the report. Out of time cases, I send Operations a list of cases that were out of time to be sure they were really out of time; sometimes there's a wrong date in MATIS that made a case out of time when it shouldn't be. In that case Operations has to update the date in MATIS and then I re run the report to get the corrected data. 15209,User Story,MCS Monthly Reports,"we have a set of scheduled monthly reports that includes: Activity Report Savings Report TAT Report Network Availability these reports are not automatically sent to the client because they need to be checked by me first to be sure that all pieces are in the right place like: Membership; I make sure that we received the eligibility file on time, sometimes we have to ask IT / Finance for it. NOTE:&nbsp; MATIS is configured for MCS with LOBs of Medicare, Medicaid, and Commercial.&nbsp; However, MCS only provides Medicare Advantage requests for our processing.&nbsp; So, there is no configuration or code in MATIS that restricts Vial rounding to just the Medicare Advantage providers as described in the attached document (requirements), we rely on the fact that MCS only submits requests for this provider/member population. Client Success (Islen) has provided MCS with an updated Authorization Request Form which includes patient height and weight fields, and an option to select vial rounding.&nbsp; MCS is training their staff to use this new form.&nbsp;&nbsp; Client Sucess (Islen) also provided MCS with the attached MCS Oncology Dose Rounding Program Communication. The MCS Rx Drug Scope list attachment was provided by the Payer Solutions team, Amber.&nbsp; It is used to upload the payer's formulary for the first time for MCS, specifying each drug's prior authorization and vial rounding status.&nbsp; Note that the prior authorization status indicators in the payer formulary do not impact case processing.&nbsp; The vial rounding status does, and is applicable to treatment date spans, with the vial rounding status drug's optional rounding being effective 07/24/2023. MATIS' MCS payer configuration must be updated to require height and weight for this solution. No notification letter changes are required. CRS will be required to input the height and weight and select the vial rounding consideration option before closing a case. 166051,User Story,"MATIS DB, BE Investigate outdated AssignmentProvider trigger removal","Tech Debt The trigger on AssignmentProvider info table was added in the 2020, once multi-provider assignment to case was implemented (added treating provider, PoT). Though, that time much code was dependent on Provider fields in the PatientProtocolAssignment table. So, decision was made to sync those fields automatically from AssignmentProvider, once it is updated. That worked fine, though was very unobvious, until Source was introduced. Trigger was not updated with it, so it worked with bug for 4 years, until issued was diagnosed in scope of SSO Provider project (for BCBSMI, Sentara). The impact was serious - PPA.CurrentMDID field could be assigned with OrderingProvider belonging to other Source. The issue was fixed in Q1 2025 by introducing the Source filtering in the trigger. It just added another hard-coded mapping between Source and Payer. That will create a problem with maintenance of such code in the future, since developers who are unaware about this trigger will normally skip updating this mapping table. And that may cause silent and extremely hard to diagnose issue on PROD.",,2025-08-11 23:11:44,Closed,Technology\Engineering\Dragons,Technology\2025 Q3\25.08.27,Committed,162154,P32 Health | Medicare Expansion | External Case Entry,P32,Provider & Network Management,Data | Provider Data,Baseline Implementation

10903,User Story,Appeal Process Assignment: Add Fields to Appeals and Assignment Sections,"The purpose of this field is to capture the date and time the written final determination notification is sent to the patient or patient representative. &nbsp; For SSS, the CRS will manually add the provider and/or member approval and denial letters to the SSS Asset/Vital system and the client will send the letters to their mail vendor. Therefore, the fields will capture the dates OA copies the documents to SSS's system. For all other payers, the fields will contain the date/time the mail vendor sent the document.&nbsp; This user story will add the new fields and modify the Notification Date/Time field on Part C or Part D assignment types &lt;&gt; appeal.&nbsp; Modifying appeals will occurring during a future release. &nbsp; Per the CMS regulations: &quot;If an enrollee has identified a representative, all notices or other correspondence that must be sent to the enrollee per the regulations at 42 CFR Part 422 or 423 Subpart M must be sent to the enrollee s representative instead of to the enrollee. Plans may send notices or correspondence to both the representative and enrollee, but are not required.&quot; Also, the enrollee can call and request to have the information sent to on an ad hoc basis.&nbsp; Therefore, 2 fields are being added to the UI.&nbsp; IN ORDER TO display the assignment and appeal fields in their own sections add a read only 'Patient Notification Date/Time' and Patient Rep. Notification Date/Time field assignment section and add editable ones to the appeals section. THE SYSTEM SHOULD behave in a manner that allows the delineation of assignment and appeals fields on the Appeals Process Assignment and shows new patient and patient rep. fields SO THAT the users can clearly identify assignment and appeal fields. users know when the written member appeal and assignment notification was sent to the patient or patient representative. 138611,User Story,Sentara DMAS Report,"DMAS Specifications: Filters Only Virginia state plan of issue should be reported Only Medicaid should be reported Only pharmacy benefit drugs should be reported Only approvals and denials should be reported The report will be filtered by reporting period by using the provider written notification date There are three scenarios that will be reported: Scenario 1: Case is created, decided, and notified to the provider with no supplemental information (NMI) Scenario 2: Case is created. Supplemental information is requested (NMI sent). Supplemental information is received (NMI received) before the provider notification Scenario 3: Case is created. Supplemental information is requested (NMI sent). Supplemental information is NOT received (NMI received) before the provider notification Special things to note: Since this report is by drug and DMAS expects unique case numbers for each drug, the OA reference number will need to have an incrementing suffix on it. For example, if the OA reference number is 12345, it should suffix 12345-01 for the first drug on the case and 12345-02 for the second drug on the case, etc. Here are the requirements to report the three scenarios: Scenario 1 - This is when there are no NMIs sent There will be one row per drug per case on the report Each field should be populated with the following information: Service Authorization Identifier - This will be the OA Reference number with the suffix mentioned above Medicaid ID - This will be the member's Medicaid ID Service Auth Type - This will be &quot;A&quot; if the drug was approved and &quot;D&quot; if the drug was denied Date service auth was received by MCO - This is the received date of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Time service auth was received by MCO - This is the received Time of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Date response sent to provider - This is the provider notification date of the decision Time response sent to provider - This is the provider notification Time of the decision MCO Response Time - The is the difference between the date received and the provider notification date, in hh:mm:ss Drug product name - This is the name of the drug we are doing the authorization for Generic name of the requested product - This will be the same as the drug product name since we don't have a good way to get the generic name Drug strength - This will be the strength and dose form Resubmission indicator - This will be &quot;N&quot; Scenario 2 - This is when there is an NMI sent and an NMI received There will be two rows per drug per case on the report The first row for each drug will contain information from receipt date to NMI sent date Each field should be populated with the following information: Service Authorization Identifier - This will be the OA Reference number with the suffix mentioned above Medicaid ID - This will be the member's Medicaid ID Service Auth Type - This will be &quot;S&quot;, stating supplemental information was requested Date service auth was received by MCO - This is the received date of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Time service auth was received by MCO - This is the received Time of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Date response sent to provider - This is the date the first NMI was sent out Time response sent to provider - This is the time the first NMI was sent out MCO Response Time - The is the difference between the date received and the NMI sent date, in hh:mm:ss Drug product name - This is the name of the drug we are doing the authorization for Generic name of the requested product - This will be the same as the drug product name since we don't have a good way to get the generic name Drug strength - This will be the strength and dose form Resubmission indicator - This will be &quot;N&quot; The second row for each drug will contain information from NMI received date to provider notification date of the decision Each field should be populated with the following information: Service Authorization Identifier - This will be the OA Reference number with the suffix mentioned above Medicaid ID - This will be the member's Medicaid ID Service Auth Type - This will be &quot;A&quot; if the drug was approved and &quot;D&quot; if the drug was denied Date service auth was received by MCO - This is the date the last NMI was received that is before the provider notification date Time service auth was received by MCO - This is the time the last NMI was received that is before the provider notification date Date response sent to provider - This is the provider notification date of the decision Time response sent to provider - This is the provider notification time of the decision MCO Response Time - The is the difference between the NMI received date and the provider notification date, in hh:mm:ss Drug product name - This is the name of the drug we are doing the authorization for Generic name of the requested product - This will be the same as the drug product name since we don't have a good way to get the generic name Drug strength - This will be the strength and dose form Resubmission indicator - This will be &quot;Y&quot; Scenario 3 - This is when there is an NMI sent and no NMI received There will be two rows per drug per case on the report The first row for each drug will contain information from receipt date to NMI sent date Each field should be populated with the following information: Service Authorization Identifier - This will be the OA Reference number with the suffix mentioned above Medicaid ID - This will be the member's Medicaid ID Service Auth Type - This will be &quot;S&quot;, stating supplemental information was requested Date service auth was received by MCO - This is the received date of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Time service auth was received by MCO - This is the received Time of the case. It will be either the payer receipt date or assignment initiation date, whichever is first Date response sent to provider - This is the date the first NMI was sent out Time response sent to provider - This is the time the first NMI was sent out MCO Response Time - The is the difference between the date received and the NMI sent date, in hh:mm:ss Drug product name - This is the name of the drug we are doing the authorization for Generic name of the requested product - This will be the same as the drug product name since we don't have a good way to get the generic name Drug strength - This will be the strength and dose form Resubmission indicator - This will be &quot;N&quot; The second row for each drug will contain information from NMI sent date to provider notification date of the decision Each field should be populated with the following information: Service Authorization Identifier - This will be the OA Reference number with the suffix mentioned above Medicaid ID - This will be the member's Medicaid ID Service Auth Type - This will be &quot;A&quot; if the drug was approved and &quot;D&quot; if the drug was denied Date service auth was received by MCO - This is the date the first NMI was sent Time service auth was received by MCO - &nbsp; This is the time the first NMI was sent Date response sent to provider - &nbsp; 139814,User Story,Sentara Engg TAT Enhancements for RequestMissingInfo Deadline to support DMAS requirements,"To support medicaid for Sentara we need to provide two separate timers: Start of case to first instance of NMI (if it occurs). Either: First instance of NMI to case complete (if it occurred), OR&nbsp; Start of case to case complete These are already very close with what already have (respectively: 1 RequestMissingInfoDeadline; 2 PostmarkDeadline)&nbsp; BUT with the following exceptions: RequestMissingInfoDeadline is allowed to go OOTF instead of canceling PostmarkDeadline starts when an NMI occurs To support the above we need to do the following: Create a new Timer Type that is allowed to go OOTF called: RequestMissingInfoDeadlineOOTFAllowed It should have the following configuration that should be similar to the RequestMissingInfoDeadline Timer Type: &nbsp; (Only applies to Commercial and Medicaid LOB.) Reopen cases are&nbsp; are new cases generated in MATIS using the same drugs from the originally denied case and the template used is the approval template if the reopen outcome is &quot;overturned&quot;.&nbsp; If a previously denied request is submitted for reopen and the result is a Reopen Overturn, the approval letter is sent to member and provider. (Only applies to Medicare LOB.) Partial approval is when a case is&nbsp; approved , but at lower dose, duration or quantity.&nbsp; Clinical trial cases are administratively approved and the template used is the approval template. Provider notifications are preceded by a fax cover page. Reopen cases are new cases generated in M ATIS using the same GMT codes from the originally denied case and the template used is the approval template &nbsp; if &nbsp; the reconsideration outcome is &quot;overturned&quot; .&nbsp; Upon review of the Reopen Workflow, it looks as if the letters cannot be tested.&nbsp; They will be descoped from QA, but letters should be created. === SHEET: Dataset Overview ===
Dataset Overview,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,
"Scope: 2,916 total work items spanning May 24, 2018 – October 9, 2025, covering baseline platform development and multiple client implementations.

Work Item Types:
 - Baseline Implementation: 2,276 items (78.1%) – core product functionality and initial rollout work.
 - Client Customization: 640 items (21.9%) – client-specific modifications or extensions of baseline features.

Domains: Work spans 11 functional domains. The largest are: Formulary & Pharmacy Data (19%) and Provider & Network Management (18%), jointly representing ~37% of all items, followed by Eligibility & Member Data (15%) . 
Unclassified domain/feature represents items pending proper domain tagging.

Features:
18 feature categories captured. Most activity centers around:
- Engg | Business Rules – frequent rule and configuration updates.
- Data | Provider Data and Data | Pharmacy Auths + PBM Integrations – key integration areas.
- Data | Eligibility Data and Engg | Notifications – common customization targets.

Clients: 13 clients represented. Top by volume: Geisinger (567), BCBSM (414), MCS (297), Sentara (289), and MMM (262) -  indicating heavier customization or implementation scope for these payers.

Common Keywords in Custom Work Items: Text analysis of customization tickets reveals consistent themes across clients. Most custom work centers on workflow modifications, integration adjustments, rule configuration, and notifications. Frequent keywords include workflow, integration, rules, authorization, notification, data mapping, and letter templates—indicating that clients often need unique workflows, system interfaces, business rules, and communication outputs. These patterns point to recurring customization motifs:
- System Integrations with client-specific APIs and data feeds.
- usiness Rule Adjustments reflecting payer policy variations.
- Workflow Tweaks such as added review steps or routing logic.
- Notification & Letter Customization for branding and communication preferences.
- Occasional UI Changes (branding or field visibility).",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,

=== SHEET: Domain  Functional Area Insight ===
Domain / Functional Area,% Items that are Custom,Top Features Customized,Clients Affected,Key Observations
Formulary & Pharmacy Data,26.5% (147 of 554),"Pharmacy Auths + PBM Integrations, Formulary Data feeds",7+ clients,"High variability – each client’s formulary and PBM integration needed changes. Common custom work includes mapping drug codes, adjusting auth rules, and syncing with various PBM systems. This domain has the highest custom count, signaling a need for a more flexible integration framework for pharmacy data."
Provider & Network Management,25.4% (134 of 527),"Provider Data, Network Checks (GAP review)",6+ clients,"Significant customizations around provider directory imports, credentialing workflows, and network gap checks. Different clients have unique provider data sources and referral rules, driving one - quarter of tasks to be custom. Standardizing provider data exchange formats could reduce custom efforts here."
Eligibility & Member Data,16.2% (71 of 438),Eligibility Data,5+ clients,Moderate variability; many customizations deal with eligibility file formats and membership validation rules. Several clients required custom parsers for eligibility feeds. A generic eligibility ingestion module or mapping tool might address these variations.
Reporting & Analytics,8.1% (25 of 310),"Analytics Jobs, Reporting Framework",4+ clients,"Relatively low customization. Most reporting needs are met by baseline solutions; a few clients had custom reporting jobs or analytics scripts (perhaps specific dashboards or data extracts). The lower custom % suggests reports are largely standardized, with minor bespoke analytics requests."
Configuration & Rules Engine,19.3% (56 of 290),"Business Rules, Payer Configurations",6+ clients,"One - fifth of work in this domain is custom, reflecting how each payer’s policies necessitate unique rule configurations. Top custom features include adding client-specific rule sets or extending the rules engine for new scenario types. Indicates strong need for a dynamic rules configuration capability to minimize code changes."
Notifications & Alerts,18.5% (45 of 243),"Chemo/XRT Notifications, Letter Templates",5+ clients,"Customizations often involve tailoring notification content and triggers (e.g., customizing letter templates, adding notification channels). Multiple clients requested their own letter formats and notification criteria. A template-driven notification system could allow non-code customization (e.g., editing templates or rules per client)."
Claims & Enterprise Data,12.8% (23 of 179),Claims Data integration,3–4 clients,"Relatively lower customization. Some custom efforts to integrate with client claim systems or enterprise data warehouses (like custom claim status checks or data exports). Many clients use similar claims standards, so baseline suffices in most cases."
Third-Party Integrations,21.8% (36 of 165),3rd Party IVR/Portal Integrations,4+ clients,"High customization ratio, as expected – each third-party integration (e.g., phone IVR systems, external provider portals) often requires custom development. Top customizations involve adapting to unique APIs or data formats for these external systems. Emphasizes the benefit of a plug-in architecture for integrations."
Admin & Operations,9.7% (14 of 144),Admin Console Enhancements,2–3 clients,"Low customization. Admin tools and operational features are mostly uniform, with a handful of client requests for additional admin console features or custom permission configurations. This domain shows high reuse of baseline functionality."
Platform Infrastructure,5.8% (8 of 139),"DevOps/Deployment, Monitoring jobs",2 clients,"Very low customization – platform infrastructure (deployment scripts, environment setup, monitoring) is largely standardized across clients. Only minor client-specific infra tasks (e.g., custom deployment for on-premise vs cloud, if any) are noted."
Unclassified (Review Needed),21.3% (27 of 127),(Varies – integration and misc.),4+ clients,A sizable fraction of these uncategorized items are custom. They likely pertain to cross-domain or miscellaneous tasks such as environment configurations or data fixes that weren’t classified. Many have tags suggesting integration or data migration work. This may indicate missing categories in the domain taxonomy (see Section 6 for details).
,,,,
,,,,
"Key Domain Trends: Domains dealing with external data (Formulary, Provider, Integrations) have the highest customization percentages (20–26%), reinforcing that differences in external partner systems and data formats drive custom development. Domains representing internal platform components (Admin, Infrastructure) have <10% custom work, implying those modules are one-size-fits-all. Domains like Eligibility and Notifications are intermediate (~15–19%), showing some variation but often manageable via baseline configuration (with some exceptions needing code tweaks).

",,,,
,,,,
"Payer Variability: Domains like Formulary/Pharmacy and Provider Management affect a broad range of clients (at least 6–7 out of 13 clients had custom items in those domains). This suggests virtually every client had something unique in how they manage formularies or provider networks. On the other hand, domains like Claims Data or Admin had customizations in fewer clients (only a few specific clients needed something extra), indicating that either those clients had special use cases or others were satisfied with baseline.",,,,
